reforms.description: The government has initiated the creation of special zones where key regulations are abolished or modified on a trial basis to enhance local autonomy and stimulate innovation. This initiative aims to stimulate regulatory competition among municipalities to attract new business activities, including research and development, by allowing local governments to propose reforms for approval.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing local autonomy and stimulating innovation through regulatory competition, the initiative is expected to raise productivity and attract new business activities.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiative has been actively implemented to stimulate innovation.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a structural change aimed at enhancing innovation through regulatory modifications.
reforms.source_quote: The special zone initiative has also helped to increase transparency; in many cases, local authorities have asked for the elimination of regulations that do not exist.

reforms.description: The reduction of capital requirements for new start-ups in 2003 from 3 million yen to only 1 yen contributed to a 10% rise in the number of newly created companies, facilitating entrepreneurship and innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Lowering capital requirements encourages entrepreneurship, leading to increased business formation and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The reduction in capital requirements has been successfully implemented, resulting in a rise in new startups.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform significantly impacts the startup ecosystem by facilitating the creation of new companies.
reforms.source_quote: The reduction of the capital requirements for new start-ups in 2003 from 3 million yen to only 1 yen contributed to a 10 percent rise in the number of newly created companies.
